prompt: A clean claim under Medicare generally means:

options.text: Complete, accurate, timely submission with required identifiers and no missing info

explanation: Clean claims contain all data needed for adjudication without additional information.

prompt: CMS-1500 form is primarily used to bill:
explanation: CMS-1500 is the professional claim form; UB-04 is institutional.

prompt: Place of service (POS) code 11 indicates:
explanation: POS 11 is office setting on professional claims.

prompt: Claim rejection at clearinghouse for invalid subscriber ID should be:

options.text: Billed to patient without verification always first
Corrected with verified insurance information and resubmitted

explanation: Rejections are pre-adjudication edits fixed by correcting data and resubmitting.

prompt: Coordination of benefits (COB) applies when patient has:

options.text: Primary and secondary coverage; primary pays first per rules
Only workers' comp for unrelated cold visit always

explanation: COB determines order of payment when multiple payers cover the patient.

distractorExplanations.c: Unrelated injury may not be WC.

prompt: Timely filing limit denial means:

options.text: Claim submitted after payer deadline without valid exception

explanation: Each payer sets filing limits; late claims deny unless appeal exception applies.

distractorExplanations.d: Valid NPI would not cause this denial.

prompt: Advance Beneficiary Notice (ABN) is used when:

options.text: Medicare may not pay for service and patient chooses to proceed
Coding is always correct without discussion

explanation: ABN documents patient liability when Medicare coverage unlikely.

prompt: Electronic remittance advice (ERA/835) provides:

options.text: Explanation of payment, adjustments, and reason codes

explanation: 835 ERA details paid amounts, adjustments (CARC/RARC), and claim status.

prompt: Duplicate claim denial often results from:

options.text: Same service billed twice with same date/provider without modifier/resubmission indicator
Corrected claim with appropriate frequency code

explanation: Payers detect duplicate service lines; use frequency/modifier for corrections/voids.

prompt: Global surgical package includes:

options.text: Pre-op visit day before major surgery, surgery, and follow-up within global period per CPT
New problem visit same day always separately paid without modifier
Anesthesia always in global for all payers without rules

explanation: Global periods bundle certain pre/post op services; separate billing rules apply.

distractorExplanations.c: Same-day E/M may need modifier 25 if significant separate problem.

prompt: Claim scrubber edit 'missing referring provider NPI' for specialist visit should be fixed by:

options.text: Adding valid referring/ordering provider when required by payer

explanation: Many plans require referring provider for specialist claims.

prompt: Void/correct claim process typically uses:

options.text: Phone call only without resubmission ever
Frequency code 8 void then new claim or frequency 7 replacement per payer rules

explanation: Electronic claims use frequency/type codes to void or replace prior submission.

distractorExplanations.c: Claim and payment systems must align.

prompt: Patient statement after insurance payment should show:

options.text: Service detail, insurance payments/adjustments, patient responsibility per EOB alignment

explanation: Statements reflect allowed amounts, payments, adjustments, and remaining patient liability.

prompt: Workers' compensation claim billing generally requires:

options.text: Medicare primary always for work injury
WC payer info, injury date, employer, and diagnosis linked to work injury

explanation: WC claims need injury-related data and often treat authorization per state.

distractorExplanations.a: WC usually primary for work injury.

prompt: Underbilling same service repeatedly below contracted rate by error should be:

options.text: Hidden to avoid payer attention forever
Corrected through rebilling/contract review and internal audit
Considered fraud if intentional overbilling only—underbilling never matters

explanation: Accurate billing reflects contract; patterns affect revenue and compliance audits.

prompt: ICD-10-CM codes primarily describe:

options.text: Diagnoses, symptoms, and reasons for encounter

explanation: ICD-10-CM captures diagnosis and clinical status supporting medical necessity.

prompt: CPT code 99213 typically represents:
explanation: 99213 is common established patient E/M level based on MDM/time per rules.

prompt: Modifier 25 indicates:

options.text: Significant, separately identifiable E/M on same day as procedure

explanation: Modifier 25 supports separate E/M with procedure same day when criteria met.

prompt: Upcoding means:

options.text: Billing correct level with full documentation
Billing higher level service than documented/supported

explanation: Upcoding is fraudulent misrepresentation of service intensity.

prompt: Medical necessity requires:

options.text: Patient preference alone without clinical indication
Service reasonable and necessary for diagnosis/treatment supported in record

explanation: Payers deny services not justified by diagnosis and clinical documentation.

prompt: HCPCS Level II codes often represent:

options.text: Supplies, drugs, ambulance, and DME not in CPT

explanation: HCPCS includes J-codes for drugs, A-codes ambulance, etc.

prompt: Laterality in ICD-10 (left/right) should:

options.text: Match clinical documentation specifying side when applicable
Always unspecified if unsure without query

explanation: Specificity including laterality improves accuracy; query provider if documentation unclear.

distractorExplanations.c: Unspecified only if truly unknown after query.

prompt: NCCI edits prevent:

options.text: Improper unbundling of component codes billed separately when bundled
Correct coding with modifiers when allowed

explanation: National Correct Coding Initiative pairs codes that should not bill together unless modifier appropriate.

distractorExplanations.b: Modifiers may override when clinically appropriate.

prompt: Principal diagnosis on inpatient claim is:

options.text: Billing clerk choice without coder review
Condition established after study to be chiefly responsible for admission

explanation: Inpatient principal diagnosis drives DRG assignment after physician/coder validation.

distractorExplanations.c: Z codes are factors/status when applicable.

prompt: Evaluation and management leveling uses (2021+ office/outpatient rules emphasize):

options.text: Medical decision making and/or total time on date of encounter per documentation

explanation: E/M levels based on MDM elements or time per current CMS/AMA guidelines.

prompt: External cause codes (V/W/X/Y) capture:

options.text: Mechanism/circumstance of injury or poisoning when documented

explanation: External cause codes supplement injury/poisoning coding when documented.

distractorExplanations.d: Specialty on claim field not external cause.

prompt: Unlisted CPT code requires:

options.text: Description and often comparable code with documentation supporting unique service

explanation: Unlisted codes need report/comparison to establish value and medical necessity.

distractorExplanations.d: Routine visits have specific E/M codes.

prompt: Modifier 59 indicates:

options.text: Distinct procedural service when no more specific modifier applies

explanation: 59 breaks NCCI bundles when separate session/site/organ/encounter per criteria.

prompt: Query physician when documentation:

options.text: Conflicts or lacks specificity needed for accurate code assignment
Matches nursing note only without conflict

explanation: Clinical documentation improvement queries resolve ambiguity before coding.

prompt: SNOMED in EHR differs from ICD-10 on claim because:

options.text: Claims require ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes; SNOMED supports clinical documentation mapping

explanation: SNOMED CT is clinical terminology; billing uses ICD-10-CM mapped from documentation.

prompt: Medicare Part A primarily covers:

options.text: Inpatient hospital, skilled nursing (limited), hospice, some home health

explanation: Part A is hospital insurance for inpatient/SNF/hospice benefits when eligible.

prompt: Medicare Part B generally covers:

options.text: Physician services, outpatient care, DME, preventive services

explanation: Part B is medical insurance for outpatient professional/services and DME.

prompt: Medicare Advantage (Part C) claims are submitted to:

options.text: The Medicare Advantage plan, not Original Medicare directly
Medicaid always first for all services

explanation: MA plans are paid capitation; providers bill the plan per contract.

prompt: Medicaid eligibility and billing vary by:

options.text: State rules, managed care plans, and fee schedules

explanation: Medicaid is state-administered with federal guidelines; billing rules differ.

prompt: TRICARE is health coverage for:

options.text: Only veterans at VA exclusively always billed same
Uniformed service members, retirees, and families

explanation: TRICARE serves military community; billing follows TRICARE rules distinct from VA direct care.

prompt: Commercial payer prior authorization denial for MRI should prompt:

options.text: Bill Medicare if patient has none
Review denial reason, peer-to-peer if available, patient notification, alternative plan

explanation: Appeals and peer review may overturn; patient informed of options and liability.

prompt: Medicare secondary payer (MSP) rules may make Medicare secondary when:

options.text: Group health plan due to active employment over certain size/age rules or other insurance primary situations
Patient has no other coverage ever

explanation: MSP provisions determine when Medicare pays secondary to other insurance.

distractorExplanations.b: No other coverage means Medicare primary if eligible.
distractorExplanations.d: Screening has specific rules but MSP broader.

prompt: Capitated contract means provider:

options.text: Billed fee-for-service only per visit always
Receives fixed per-member payment and manages care within budget

explanation: Capitation pays per enrolled member period regardless of visit volume.

prompt: Explanation of Benefits (EOB) to patient shows:

options.text: What provider billed, allowed amount, plan paid, patient owes—not a bill itself usually

explanation: EOB explains adjudication; provider statement may follow for patient balance.

prompt: Blue Cross plan out-of-state emergency care often:

options.text: Paid only if provider in Antarctica
Processed under emergency/urgent rules per member contract and federal protections

explanation: Emergency services have special network/payment rules under ACA and plan terms.

distractorExplanations.a: Emergencies generally covered with cost share.
distractorExplanations.c: Geography joke; real rules apply domestically.

prompt: Medicare Part D is:

options.text: Prescription drug coverage through private plans

explanation: Part D plans cover outpatient drugs via PDP/MA-PD.

prompt: Railroad Medicare claims are processed by:

options.text: Palmetto GBA or designated MAC under special Medicare rules for railroad beneficiaries

explanation: Railroad Retirement Board beneficiaries have Medicare processed through designated contractors.

prompt: Self-funded employer plan claims are paid by:

options.text: Employer/plan administrator often using TPA; not fully insured carrier risk

explanation: Self-funded ERISA plans use administrator; billing follows plan documents.

distractorExplanations.a: May coordinate but employer plan primary per rules.

prompt: HMO referral requirement means:

options.text: Any provider nationwide always without ID
PCP referral/authorization needed for specialist except emergencies per plan

explanation: HMOs gatekeep specialty care through PCP/referrals and network.

prompt: Patient eligibility verification should occur:

options.text: Before service when possible and at intervals for ongoing treatment

explanation: Real-time eligibility reduces denials for termed coverage or changed benefits.

prompt: HIPAA Privacy Rule requires:

options.text: Safeguarding PHI, minimum necessary use/disclosure, patient rights
Sharing SSN in waiting room aloud

explanation: HIPAA mandates administrative, physical, technical safeguards and patient rights.

prompt: False Claims Act exposure occurs when:

options.text: Providing free charity care policy compliant
Knowingly submitting false claims for government payment

explanation: FCA imposes liability for fraudulent government healthcare claims.

prompt: OIG compliance program elements include:

options.text: Written policies, training, auditing, enforcement, and response procedures

explanation: Federal guidance expects seven elements for effective compliance programs.

prompt: Stark Law generally prohibits:

options.text: Physician self-referral for designated health services to entities with financial relationship absent exception

explanation: Stark restricts DHS referrals where physician has financial relationship unless exception met.

prompt: Anti-Kickback Statute concerns:

options.text: Paying fair market value rent with safe harbor
Remuneration to induce referrals for services paid by federal programs
Discounts fully compliant with safe harbor when structured properly

explanation: AKS criminalizes inducements for federal healthcare referrals; safe harbors protect lawful arrangements.

distractorExplanations.a: FMV rent safe harbor may protect.

prompt: Documentation retention for claims generally requires:

options.text: Destroy all records day after payment
Maintaining records supporting services billed per payer/regulatory periods (often 5–7+ years)
Patient keeps only copy provider destroys immediately

explanation: Retention policies follow CMS/state/false claims standards typically multi-year.

distractorExplanations.a: Destruction too soon violates audit ability.

prompt: RAC audit targets:

options.text: Medicare improper payments through review of claims and medical necessity

explanation: Recovery Audit Contractors identify overpayments/underpayments on Medicare claims.

prompt: Credit card on file for copay collection must follow:

options.text: PCI-DSS security and patient authorization/disclosure policies
Storing CVV in plain text spreadsheet shared

explanation: Payment card data requires secure processing and transparent patient consent.

prompt: Incident response for ransomware on billing system includes:

options.text: Pay ransom silently without reporting always legal
Isolate systems, notify leadership/IT, assess breach notification obligations, restore from backups
Ignore and continue billing on infected machines only
Post PHI online to prove hack

explanation: Cyber incidents require containment, forensic analysis, and potential HIPAA breach notification.

distractorExplanations.a: Reporting and legal review still needed.

prompt: Billing unbundled lab panels intentionally for higher pay is:

options.text: Fraud/abuse if panels should be billed as panel per policy

explanation: Deliberate unbundling violating NCCI/payer rules is compliance violation.

distractorExplanations.a: Panels bill as panel when appropriate.

prompt: Compliance hotline purpose:
explanation: Hotlines support reporting without retaliation per policy.

prompt: Excluded individual on OIG LEIE list:

options.text: Cannot participate in federal healthcare programs; hiring/billing prohibited

explanation: LEIE lists individuals/entities excluded from Medicare/Medicaid participation.

prompt: Balanced Billing (surprise billing) protections under No Surprises Act generally limit:

options.text: All medical care worldwide forever free
Out-of-network cost sharing in certain emergency and facility settings

explanation: NSA restricts surprise OON bills in specified settings with IDR process.

prompt: Release of information for billing to attorney requires:

options.text: Patient authorization compliant with HIPAA/state law specifying scope
Billing clerk personal decision only without form

explanation: Attorney access needs valid HIPAA authorization or legal process.

prompt: Regular compliance training for billing staff should document:

options.text: Attendance, topics covered, and periodic updates on fraud/waste/abuse and HIPAA

explanation: Documented training demonstrates compliance program effectiveness to auditors.
